back_translation: Then the LORD answered Job out of the whirlwind and said:
'Who is this who darkens My counsel with words without knowledge?
Now gird up your loins like a man; I will question you, and you shall inform Me.
Where were you when I laid the foundations of the earth? Tell Me, if you have understanding.
Who fixed its measurements? Surely you know! Or who stretched a measuring line across it?
On what were its foundations set, or who laid its cornerstone,
while the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Who enclosed the sea behind doors when it burst forth from the womb,
when I made the clouds its garment and thick darkness its swaddling band,
when I fixed its boundaries and set its bars and doors in place,
and I declared, 'You may come this far, but no farther; here your proud waves must stop'?
In all your days, have you commanded the morning to come or assigned the dawn its place,
that it might take hold of the edges of the earth and shake the wicked out of it?
The earth is transformed like clay under a seal, and the hills stand out like the folds of a garment.
Light is withheld from the wicked, and their upraised arm is broken.
Have you traveled to the springs of the sea or walked in the trenches of the deep?
Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Have you seen the gates of the shadow of death?
Have you surveyed the vast expanse of the earth? Tell Me, if you know all this.
Where is the way to the dwelling of light, and where does darkness reside,
that you could lead it back to its territory and know the paths to its home?
Surely you know, for you were already born, and the number of your days is great!
Have you entered the storehouses of snow, or seen the storehouses of hail,
which I have reserved for times of trouble, for the day of war and battle?
Where is the way the lightning is dispersed, or the east wind scattered over the earth?
Who cuts a channel for the rain, or a path for the thunderbolt,
to bring rain on a land with no one in it, on a desert where no man lives,
to satisfy the parched and desolate ground and make the tender grass sprout?
Does the rain have a father? Who gives birth to the drops of dew?
From whose womb does the ice come, and who gives birth to the frost of heaven,
when the waters become hard as stone and the surface of the deep freezes over?
Can you bind the chains of the Pleiades, or loosen the belt of Orion?
Can you bring out the constellations in their seasons, or lead out the Bear with her cubs?
Do you know the laws of the heavens? Can you establish their rule over the earth?
Can you raise your voice to the clouds so that a flood of water covers you?
Can you send out the lightning bolts so that they go? Do they report to you, 'Here we are'?
Who put wisdom in the heart, or gave understanding to the mind?
Who has the wisdom to count the clouds, or who can pour out the water jars of heaven,
when the dust hardens into a mass and the clods of earth stick together?
Can you hunt prey for the lioness or satisfy the hunger of young lions,
when they crouch in their dens and lie in wait in the thicket?
Who provides food for the raven when its young cry out to God and wander about for lack of food?
